hooks.prompt: Output nothing if safe. Only output if the command contains: git reset --hard, git push --force, git push -f, rm -rf, DROP TABLE, DROP DATABASE, git clean -fd, or DELETE FROM without a WHERE clause. If dangerous: one line only — '⚠️ DANGEROUS: [what will be lost] — confirm before running.'

Output nothing unless a check fails. If file path does NOT contain /src/ or /tests/ or /database/ — output nothing. If it does, check: (1) new FUNCTION declarations (lines starting with 'export function', 'export async function', 'export default function', or 'const X = (' where X is the exported name of a function component or hook) are missing JSDoc — variable declarations like 'const x = value' do NOT require JSDoc, (2) console logs use emoji prefix, (3) new env vars are in .env.example. If a check fails: one line only — '⚠️ [what failed] in [file]'. If all pass: output nothing.
If code was written this session, output exactly one line: '📝 Run /wrap to save session progress.' Otherwise output nothing.
